response:
The Kingdom of Bohemia, also referred to as the **Czech Kingdom**, was the name given to the medieval and early modern monarchy in Central Europe that included the modern Czech Republic. It was sometimes called the **České království** in Czech, **Königreich Böhmen** in German, and **Regnum Bohemiae** in Latin.

Thus, **Jaroslav Kvapil the Czech poet was born in the Kingdom of Bohemia, also referred to by the name Czech Kingdom**.
